asctl is a CLI for managing App Store Connect: TestFlight, builds, releases,
code signing, team users, and sales reports.

It wraps the App Store Connect API around real release workflows: invite and
sync beta testers, assign builds to groups, submit versions for review, and
inspect what is live — from a terminal, script, or CI job.

Basic workflow:

  asctl auth init
  asctl apps use com.example.myapp
  asctl beta groups create alpha
  asctl beta testers sync --group alpha --file testers.csv
  asctl builds assign 42 --group alpha

Guide: https://github.com/vedanta/asctl

Usage:
  asctl [command]

Health & inventory:
  help        Help about any command
  version     Show CLI version

Flags:
      --all              fetch all pages
      --app string       app context (bundle ID or ID); overrides the profile default
      --config string    path to config file (default ~/.config/asctl/config.yaml)
      --debug            log requests and responses to stderr
      --dry-run          preview what would change; touch nothing
  -h, --help             help for asctl
      --limit int        maximum number of results to return
      --no-color         disable terminal colors
  -o, --output string    output format: table, json, csv, yaml (default "table")
      --profile string   profile to use
  -q, --quiet            suppress non-essential output
      --timeout int      request timeout in seconds (default 30)
  -v, --verbose          show more detail
  -y, --yes              apply without asking for confirmation

Use "asctl [command] --help" for more information about a command.
